marketState: neutral - short-term weather-driven bounce inside an unreliable/mixed medium-term picture
summary: Front-month NG ($2.940) sits almost exactly at modeled fair value ($2.917, band $2.417-$3.417) with FAIR status at every horizon out to 21 days, so valuation gives no clean directional edge. Seasonality is internally conflicted: price is running well below its typical path (-13.7% YTD vs -3.4% median, which argues for mean reversion higher), yet the 4-week probHigher is only 31% and the typical 4-week move is -3.1% (arguing for further softness first) - and the dataset itself flags this week's seasonality reliability as Low (50pp spread). A sharp near-term heat/cooling-demand spike (1-7D demand +21.1% vs 5y, above the 5y max range) is pushing the front month into slight backwardation vs Sep/Oct and is the dominant short-horizon bullish force, while 8-16D demand is already moderating back toward normal (+5.5% vs 5y). Medium/long-timeframe technicals (1h/4h/1d/1w) are uniformly bearish with 50<200 EMA crosses, and storage is +184 Bcf above the 5y average after a bearish +72 Bcf build surprise, but managed money is net short at only the 35th percentile (not a crowded extreme), so the medium-term case is directional-but-low-conviction rather than clean.

longTerm.rationale: Seasonality (priority #1) is explicitly Low reliability and self-contradictory - price well below median argues for mean reversion, but 4-week probHigher of only 31% and a -3.1% typical move argue for further weakness before Sep's seasonal strength arrives inside this window. Fair value is FAIR (no edge) across all horizons out to 21 days, so with the top two priority inputs offering no clean signal, the storage surplus/bearish surprise and uniformly bearish technicals are not enough alone to justify a full-size directional bet.
longTerm.trigger: SHORT trigger: a rally into $3.05-$3.15 with continued bearish storage prints (surprise vs forecast) and the daily/weekly EMA cross staying bearish. LONG trigger: a pullback into the $2.55-$2.65 lower fair-value zone alongside a storage build coming in at/below forecast.

shortTerm.rationale: 1-7D weather demand is running +21.1% above 5y norms and above the 5y max, coinciding with front-month backwardation vs Sep/Oct (a tightness signal) and overbought/bullish momentum on the 5m/15m tape (RSI 66, price above the upper Bollinger band); price is essentially at fair value so there is little valuation headwind to a near-term push higher.
shortTerm.invalidation: A rollover in the 1-7D demand reading back toward the 5y norm, loss of the front-month backwardation, or a daily close back below $2.85 would invalidate this bounce trade - note it runs counter to the still-bearish 1h/4h/1d/1w trend, so it is treated as a tactical countertrend trade with tight risk.

keyDrivers: 1-7D cooling demand +21.1% vs 5y and above the 5y max range, driving near-term backwardation
Storage +184 Bcf vs 5y with a +72 Bcf bearish build surprise - ample medium-term supply cushion
Seasonality is internally conflicted (price -13.7% YTD vs -3.4% median, but 4wk probHigher only 31%) and flagged Low reliability
Fair value status is FAIR at every horizon (now through +21D) - no valuation edge either direction
Technicals uniformly bearish on 1h/4h/1d/1w (50<200 EMA cross on every timeframe) while 4h/1d RSI are oversold
risks: 8-16D weather demand is already moderating back toward normal (+5.5% vs 5y), which could quickly deflate the near-term bounce
Wide fair-value band (~$1.00/MMBtu) and Low seasonality reliability mean any medium-term directional read rests on soft statistical edges
Daily price is already below the lower Bollinger band and oversold (RSI 35.6), raising the chance of a mean-reversion bounce that could stop out a fresh short entered too early
